query: Is the following claim accurate? "It’s ironic the name of the new Covid subvariant is ‘Kraken’ a ‘mythical beast that kills on the waves’ as it’s really 5G waves that are harming and killing people."
claim_text: It’s ironic the name of the new Covid subvariant is ‘Kraken’ a ‘mythical beast that kills on the waves’ as it’s really 5G waves that are harming and killing people.
rating: There is no evidence that 5G technology is harmful to human health. The name ‘Kraken’ was chosen by a Canadian biology professor in an effort to make it easier to distinguish between different subvariants.
note: rated 'There is no evidence that 5G technology is harmful to human health. The name ‘Kraken’ was chosen by a Canadian biology professor in an effort to make it easier to distinguish between different subvariants.' by Full Fact (ClaimReview)

query: Is the following claim accurate? "Coronavirus is a result of 5G"
claim_text: Coronavirus is a result of 5G

query: Is the following claim accurate? "Medicinal tea ‘help with’ COVID-19"
claim_text: Medicinal tea ‘help with’ COVID-19
rating: INACCURATE. There is no evidence of tea curing or alleviating the symptoms of COVID-19. In fact, according to the World Health Organization, there are no medicines that have yet been shown to prevent or cure COVID-19.
note: rated 'INACCURATE. There is no evidence of tea curing or alleviating the symptoms of COVID-19. In fact, according to the World Health Organization, there are no medicines that have yet been shown to prevent or cure COVID-19.' by FactCheckNI (ClaimReview)
